feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Connector
feed.subtitle: Cloud Connector Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: task center s4hana onpremis approve issue
entry.content:
i follwed the below sap blog to configure task center for onpremis s4hana

https://blogs.sap.com/2022/12/01/sap-task-center-setup-for-s-4hana-on-premise/

tasks retrived successfully from backend s4hana to task center also displayed fine (UUID synced in both system )

but when i try to approve or reject it display error " Response Code: 401 Message: Unauthorized Error Message: XSRF token HEAD failed with code 401 for Url http://vhspeds4ci.hec.nesr.com:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_task_spi_details/default/sap/api_task_spi_details/0001/?sap-client=110 URL: http://vhspeds4ci.hec.nesr.com:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_task_spi_details/default/sap/api_task_spi_details/0001/ Try to perform the action again. If the error persists, contact System Administrator. "

for over 1 month i try to troublshouting the issue from sap blog or by sap incident with no solution till now .

any help !

entry.title: Create Subaccount in Cloud Connector, Multi Environment
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I hope someone can help me with this issue. I am stuck here for 2 weeks allready, and I cant find a clear answer.

When i try to add a Subaccount in the Cloud Connector it doesn't work. The Subaccount is a Multi-Environment.

The below screenshot is from the BTP Cockpit.

I tried to add other Subaccounts, with NEO environment, and it works. Just not for the Multi-Environment.

If anybode can help me out there, i would be really thankful. Also if more informations are needed I can provide them.

BR,

Allen 🙂

entry.title: Getting 500: internal server error from SAP CF environment
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have one of the MTA based application which started throwing 500:Internal Server error from last week.

There were no change has been made on application ,odata service or SAP cloud connector.

This error is captured only on the network call.

SAP has suggested to use a retry mechanism, which we tried and we get 500 error we callback the function but it sometimes work but sometimes not.

Please suggest your input for this issue.

Thanks,

Sumalatha

entry.title: Cloud connector issue with Docker environment
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have installed the abap-platform-trial in docker environment by following the below URL.

Dockerhub

( https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-platform-trial#sap-cloud-connector )

Successfully installed and able to access the GUI and Fiori along with Cloud connector. But, getting the exception while trying to add the BTP subaccount.

Found the issue log in the ljs_trace file.

#shell command for retrieving PID of process bound to SCC port failed

com.sap.scc.util.ShellCommand$ExecutionException: Command lsof -i :8443 failed, caused by java.io.IOException: Cannot run lsof - execvpe failed (ENOENT - No such file or directory).

Screenshot 1:

Screenshot 2:

ljs-trace.txtscc-about.txt

Thanks & Regards,

Satya.

entry.title: Which type of service can I connect for On-Premise to Cloud on SAP Cloud Connector?
entry.content: I have read some documents about the Connectivity of SAP BTP, it usually shows that On-Premise to Cloud is a connection that connects Apps, DB Tools, Dev Tools and Replication Tools to SAP HANA Cloud. I'd want to ask, "Aside from SAP HANA Cloud, are there any other services to which the On-Premise can connect?"

entry.title: SAP btp to sftp connectvity
entry.content:
Hi Team,
I am trying to achive a use case of reading a simple file placed in an sftp server. Below are the steps followed. Please help to advise as am not able to achieve the connection succesfuly.

1. Created Cloud connector with backend SFTP server. When pinged, says connectivity established.

2. Created a destination pointing to the cloud connector and the connectivty works in the console.

But I wrote my nodejs code where i first connect to connector proxy and from there to sftp server. But its says timeout. Can help to point the right way to do it.I followed this blog but of no luck

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/70715068/sftp-through-http-proxy-using-ssh2-sftp-client-library-in-node-js

Any help or sample will be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: How to call a BAPI / RFC from BTP Java Application
entry.content:
Currently I have the use case, where I need to call a BAPI / RFC in an onpremise system to adapt a users master data (like first name and SNC).

I have read in the cloud SDK Docu that this can be achieved with Java via the cloud connector. I know that the cloud sdk collegues will remove this feature in the future as they have written in the official documentation, but I still wanted to try that out (official docu: https://sap.github.io/cloud-sdk/docs/java/features/bapi-and-rfc/overview) Since it is written, that this feature will still be available for Cloud SDK version 5, I believe that it should still be available and working at the moment.

Therefore I have read how this setup can be achieved and setup a java application like the one from fukuhara here: https://blogs.sap.com/2020/05/08/how-to-call-function-modules-using-sap-cloud-sdk-for-java/comment-page-1/#comment-701698

Unfortunately this did not work for me. I always get the following error:

jakarta.servlet.ServletException: Handler dispatch failed: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/sap/conn/jco/JCoException

Becasue of this error I was wondering, if this feature is still available and can be used or if it has been removed now.?Additionally I wanted to know if there is another option to access RFC / BAPIs from a CAP application?

I have also tested a bit with node-rfc (see here: https://github.com/SAP/node-rfc), but since for Java this feature is / was built-in, I tried my luck with the first method.

Thank you very much and best regards!

entry.title: BTP and Cloud connector configuration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

1.We already have SSO configured at BTP. We are trying to add subaccount, what are the credentials to be given in subaccount user and password and how to get the credentials?


2.We have cloud connector setup and BTP subaccount is added. Later, We have registered SSO for BTP subaccount.Any changes to be done at cloud connector setup after enabling SSO for BTP?

entry.title: Cloud Connector Wild card SSL Certificate Windows Server
entry.content:
Hello, SAP gurus!

I have the following problem:

We are using wildcard SSL certificate for several things. We want to use it for the Cloud Connector as well. I was able to import the cert in the SCC UI. When trying to import it in Tomcat, I wasn't able to import the same *.pfx file. The error is "Input not an X.509 certificate". I successfully imported *.crt version of the same certificate. In the Tomcat config I uncommented


<Connector port="8443" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true"
maxParameterCount="1000
>
<SSLHostConfig>
<Certificate certificateKeystoreFile="C:\Users\Administrator\.keystore"
type="RSA" certificateKeystorePass="XXX"/>
</SSLHostConfig>
</Connector>

Cloud Connector is opening on localhost:8443, but the browser does not believe it and I have to accept the risk. Once logged in, the UI certificate is ok in the Cloud Connector.

I am wondering how to remove the certificate not valid error in the browser.

I updated to the latest SACKIT397 - Cloud Connector version 2.16.0.

entry.title: Cloud Connector test in integration suite fails
entry.content:
Hi All,

I wonder if there are any Cloud Connector exports out there that could advise me please?

I have a SAP S/4 Instance running on a Linux server that I has a 15 character hostname. I needed to create a DNS C-Name to be able to install the SAP instance due to the age old SAP long hostname restriction.

I have also installed a SAP Cloud Connector on this same Linux server so the Cloud Connector and the SAP instance are installed on the same Linux server.

When I configure the HTTP and HTTPS Virtual mappings for OnPremise in Cloud connector both of the connection tests pass OK.  I have given the subaccount in Cloud Connector a Location ID and I have connected the Cloud Connector to the Integration Suite BTP account and configured the Destination which also uses the same Location ID name and that Destination check also works fine.

But when I use the Integration Suite Cloud Connector test that fails with error: "Could not connect to Cloud Connector"

To try and simply the security I have disabled the "Determining Trust Through Allowlist" option by setting this to off.  I think this should really allow all SSL connections irrespective of SSL certificates? I have done this as the certs I download from the SAP instance use the short C-hostname I used to install SAP with.

My virtual mappings are coded with the IP address and these test OK in CC.

If I review the CC logs I do still errors about SSL certificates but for the life of me I cannot tell which one is incorrect, but if I have disabled "Determining Trust Through Allowlist" I don't think SSL certs should be causing me issues.

Would anyone have any good idea's of where I should look for my issue please?

Apologies if I am posting this in the wrong group,  this is my first time posting in the new community area and I thought I would give it a try before I raise an OSS message.

Many Thanks All

Alex

entry.title: Change Cipher suit in SAP BTP NEO tomcat
entry.content:
Hello, I have a web application deployed in SAP BTP Neo application using tomcat 9. now recently DAST scan is performed in that application . one high vulnerability is reported 

326,327 Insecure Transport: Weak SSL Cipher Inadequate Encryption Strength, Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

If I had access to server.xml of tomcat I would have done some changes but as I do not have access to it please let me know how to fix this issue. It is very urgent and I need to provide a fix ASAP. please help!

entry.title: SAP cloud connector sending huge amount of data to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a hybrid mobile application with offline storage. Its on SAP Mobile Services on SAP BTP. It has a non SAP backend exposed through SAP cloud connector. We migrated from on premise SAP Mobile Platform where it was working fine to SAP Mobile Services on cloud.

Now, the data sent between SAP CC and BTP is very high as observed by the network team. This increases upto 200GB a minute in production and causes a bottleneck and the app stops working. We were able to replicate a similar scenario where the data increases from 90MB to over 200 MB in few hours in development environment as well for a single user. Has anyone faced similar issues? 

Thanks

entry.title: Communication Arrangement Check Connection Failed
entry.content:
Hello, 

 I am following the tutorial  to read values from On premise system into ABAP Cloud.    https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-env-rfc.html

At the Step 8 Create Communication Arrangement . when i click on the Check connection. I am getting the message as The ping of the outbound service has failed.

1. In the Communication Systems --> General --> Host Name is same as the hostname that comes up in the browser when i run the transaction /ui2/flp  

 

2.  The Target Host Is same as the Destination Configuration -> URL < Host Name >

3. I checked the user credentials in the on premise system and the user credentials provided in Communication Systems --> Users for Outbound Communication  are the same. The same credentials are used in Cloud Connector also.   

4. When I Check the connection from Communication Arrangement . I am getting the error saying The ping of the outbound service has failed. In the error log it says the issue is in the service APS_COM_CA2_SRV .

Please help me resolve this issue. Also, Is there any better approach to connect to on premises S4HANA system . Thanks for your time and help.

entry.content:

I have setup and tested the Principal Propagation between my subaccount and the backend system via Cloud Connector. It seems to work fine. 

But when I try to expose an API from the system and try to test it, it says "403 - Forbidden". I am not able to troubleshoot the issue. I followed all the steps in the blog from creating instances, service keys to adding policy templates but I am not sure what is missing.

Can someone please throw some light on this? Would highly appreciate.

@shruthiarjun

entry.title: Assistance Needed: SAP Cloud Connector Connectivity Issue
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have been working on an integration between SAP ECC (On-premise) and a third-party application. As part of the on-premise integration, we aim to utilize the Cloud Connector to establish a secure connection between the SAP system and the SAP BTP Subaccount. To achieve this, we configured the Cloud Connector on our client VM machine (Windows) and connected it to the SAP BTP Subaccount by whitelisting the IPs as outlined in the SAP Official Documentation.

Recently, we encountered an issue with the Cloud Connector's connectivity to the SAP BTP Account. Upon investigation, we discovered that the IP addresses in the AWS region associated with our SAP BTP Subaccount had been updated. The documentation suggested that these updates might take effect later in Quarter Q1. Consequently, we whitelisted these new IPs in our client system. However, despite this effort, we continue to face network connection issues between the SAP BTP and the Cloud Connector.

Error:

Failed to resolve 'connectivitynotification.cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com' [A(1)]

For reference, the SAP Cloud Connector Document can be found here: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/prerequisites#loioe23f776e4d594fdbaeeb1196d47bbcc0__cf

The AWS region we are utilizing is Australia (Sydney) - AWS (cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com).

Subsequently, we opted to grant full access to all IPs without restrictions. This led to successful connection establishment. However, when we reverted to providing access only to the mentioned IP addresses of the AWS region, the connection issue resurfaced.

Could you please assist me in understanding and resolving this issue? Your guidance will be immensely valuable in identifying the root cause.

Thank you.

Prathish.

SAP Business Technology Platform 

Cloud Connector 

SAP Integration Suite

we trying to test scenario ,

we have S4H system on cloud, we need to connect to on premise content server using sap cloud connector but we don't have any documentation , we have trying the below steps
